title: Cosmos Tilt Orbs — glowing balls that roll and settle under device tilt
summary: A set of glowing orbs roll, collide and settle inside a Cosmos bowl, driven by a gravity vector {gx,gy}. Live, the vector comes from the device tilt (deviceorientation); it gracefully falls back to a synthetic oscillating tilt when the sensor is unavailable, and on desktop the mouse position becomes the tilt vector so it stays interactive. A deterministic synthetic signal drives the capture demo. Self-contained, zero-deps; privacy: all processing happens in-browser, nothing is uploaded.
usage_notes: RUN: open index.html, then tap to enable motion (iOS prompts for permission) and tilt the phone — the orbs roll toward gravity, collide and pool at the low edge of the bowl. DESKTOP fallback: no sensor needed, move the mouse and its position becomes the tilt vector. KEY TECHNIQUES: gravity vector {gx,gy} comes from deviceorientation (gamma->gx, beta->gy) or mouse, then feeds one render(SIG,t) that integrates orb velocities, resolves bowl-wall and orb-orb collisions, and draws additive radial-gradient glows; synthSig(u) = {0.7*sin(2pi*u), 0.7*cos(2pi*2u)} supplies a rocking tilt for auto and fallback so it never goes blank. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: orb count N, gravity G, restitution, bowl radius, COLS palette.
